checklist.question: Did receivables grow materially faster than revenue across consecutive periods?
Did non-current 'soft' assets (intangibles, other assets, capitalized items) grow materially faster than the business overall?

evidence.location: 10-K FY2012 income statement (revenue declined while intangibles grew 14%)

checklist.question: Is there a persistent divergence between net income and operating cash flow?

evidence.quote: NetIncomeLoss start=2015-01-01 end=2015-03-31 value=62840000 / ProfitLoss same period value=65907000

evidence.location: 10-K FY2014 comparative period, filed 2015-03-02 — ~$30M lower than originally reported FY2013 OCF

checklist.question: Do inventory levels or gross margins show trends inconsistent with revenue?

evidence.quote: No InventoryNet concept present in provided data (licensing business model, no COGS/inventory reported)
OperatingIncomeLoss start=2009-01-01 end=2009-12-31 value=152565000 on Revenues value=232058000 (65.7% margin)
OperatingIncomeLoss start=2014-01-01 end=2014-12-31 value=255833000 on Revenues value=461243000 (55.5% margin)
evidence.location: 10-K FY2014 income statement — gradual margin decline consistent with rising SG&A, not an abrupt anomaly

checklist.question: Is the earnings trajectory unusually smooth relative to the volatility of revenue and cash flow?

evidence.location: 10-K FY2014 Q4 — quarterly net income varies materially (~2.5x) across the year, not artificially smooth

checklist.question: Did liability, allowance, or reserve balances decline (or fail to grow) despite growing business activity?

evidence.quote: No allowance-for-doubtful-accounts or reserve-specific concept provided in the dataset
LiabilitiesCurrent end=2010-12-31 value=99960000; LiabilitiesCurrent end=2014-12-31 value=137128000
evidence.location: 10-Q filed 2015-05-08 — current liabilities grew modestly, no evidence of decline; total Liabilities grew from 812556000 (2010-12-31, 0001144204-12-011985) to 1804630000 (2014-12-31) largely via debt issuance, not reserve reduction

checklist.question: Does the filing chronology show irregularities (late filings/NT forms, amendments, unusual 8-K frequency)?

evidence.location: filing_chronology — sustained, multi-round SEC comment-letter correspondence over ~20 months

evidence.quote: LongTermDebtCurrent end=2014-06-30 value=666109000 vs end=2014-03-31 value=61250000 and end=2014-09-30 value=61123000
evidence.location: 10-Q Q2 2014 — one-quarter spike in current debt classification that reverses the following quarter

evidence.quote: LiabilitiesCurrent end=2014-06-30 value=732061000 vs surrounding quarters ~136000000-144000000
evidence.location: 10-Q Q2 2014 balance sheet — matching anomalous spike in current liabilities

checklist.question: Is the provided data sufficient in coverage and length for the above assessments?

evidence.quote: Quarterly and annual data spans fiscal 2008 through Q1 2015 across Revenues, NetIncomeLoss, AR, intangibles, debt, and cash flow concepts
Missing concepts: InventoryNet, AllowanceForDoubtfulAccounts, segment revenue detail limit some checks (CL4, CL6) to indirect proxies

mechanism_hypotheses.accounting_treatment: Potential premature or aggressive revenue recognition on licensing/royalty and joint-venture arrangements, evidenced by accounts receivable growing far faster than revenue in the most recent period (AR +30.8% vs revenue +6.6% in FY2014) and receivables persistently sized near a full quarter of revenue, suggesting recognition of royalty income (possibly including guaranteed minimums or JV-related revenue) ahead of collection or realizability.

Debt-funded acquisitions of brand/trademark intangibles appear to have outpaced organic revenue growth, raising risk that acquired intangible/goodwill carrying values are not supported by underlying royalty cash flows, creating latent impairment exposure not yet reflected in reported asset values.

rationale_evidence.location: 10-K cash flow financing activities — large debt issuances funding acquisitions

mechanism_hypotheses.accounting_treatment: Earnings quality/cash conversion inconsistency and balance-sheet reclassification volatility: Q1 2015 net income was roughly double operating cash flow, FY2013 operating cash flow was restated downward by ~$30M between the original and a subsequent filing, and current debt/current liabilities spiked by roughly $600M in a single quarter (Q2 2014) before reverting — patterns consistent with reclassification adjustments, working-capital timing shifts, or non-cash income components inflating reported earnings/asset balances in specific periods.

rationale_evidence.quote: NetIncomeLoss Q1 2015 value=62840000 vs NetCashProvidedByUsedInOperatingActivitiesContinuingOperations Q1 2015 value=33066000

rationale_evidence.location: 10-K FY2013 as originally filed vs restated comparative in 10-K FY2014

rationale_evidence.quote: LongTermDebtCurrent end=2014-06-30 value=666109000 (vs ~61,000,000 in adjacent quarters) and LiabilitiesCurrent end=2014-06-30 value=732061000 (vs ~136-144 million in adjacent quarters)

top_signals: Accounts receivable grew far faster than revenue in FY2014 (AR +30.8% vs revenue +6.6%), and AR remained persistently near a full quarter of royalty revenue, raising revenue-recognition/collectability concerns
Intangible assets grew +14.1% in FY2012 while revenue declined -4.3%, indicating debt-funded brand acquisitions outpacing organic growth and latent impairment risk

Sustained, multi-round SEC CORRESP/UPLOAD correspondence from November 2013 through July 2015, together with a 10-Q/A restatement (2011) and a one-quarter ~$600M spike/reversal in current debt and current liabilities (Q2 2014), suggest active regulatory scrutiny and reporting/classification instability

Q1 2015 net income was roughly double operating cash flow, and FY2013 operating cash flow was restated approximately $30M lower in a later filing, pointing to potential earnings-quality/cash-conversion issues
